questions.q: Who was Kichaka's sister?
options: Kunti
Draupadi
Sudeshna
Subhadra
questions.explanation: Kichaka was the brother of Sudeshna, the queen of King Virata of Matsya.

questions.q: Which palace servant did Kichaka pursue with unwanted advances?
options: Sairandhri, the queen's new maid
The keeper of the queen's wardrobe
The princess's dance companion
The daughter of the royal cook
questions.explanation: Kichaka was inflamed by Sairandhri, never suspecting she was Draupadi living in disguise.

questions.q: Who killed Kichaka?
options: Arjuna
Bhima
Nakula
Yudhishthira
questions.explanation: Bhima, disguised as the cook Vallabha, killed Kichaka with his bare hands in the night.

questions.q: Where did Kichaka meet his death?
options: In the royal stables at dawn
In his own quarters during a feast
On the practice grounds at noon
In the dance hall at midnight
questions.explanation: Kichaka was lured to a midnight tryst in the dance hall, where Bhima waited in the darkness.

questions.q: What position did Kichaka hold in the Matsya kingdom?
options: Chief priest of the royal household
Commander of King Virata's armies
Keeper of the royal treasury
Governor of the border provinces
questions.explanation: As commander of Virata's forces and the queen's brother, Kichaka was the real power behind the Matsya throne.

questions.q: What crucial fact did Kichaka not know about the maid he was pursuing?
options: She was a spy sent by the Kauravas
She was under Queen Sudeshna's sworn protection
She was Draupadi, and one of her five husbands was Bhima
She was a Gandharva woman wearing mortal form
questions.explanation: Sairandhri was Draupadi in disguise, and the cook working in the same palace was her husband Bhima.

questions.q: What did Bhima do to Kichaka's body after killing him?
options: Crushed it into a shapeless ball of flesh
Threw it down from the palace walls
Buried it beneath the dance hall floor
Carried it openly to the burning ground
questions.explanation: Bhima rammed Kichaka's head and limbs into his trunk, leaving a ball of flesh that terrified all who saw it.

questions.q: Who did Sairandhri claim would punish anyone who wronged her?
options: The goddess Durga whom she served
The king of the Gandharva realm
Her brothers in a distant kingdom
Her five Gandharva husbands
questions.explanation: Draupadi's cover story was that five mighty Gandharva husbands guarded her invisibly.

questions.q: What outrage did Kichaka commit in King Virata's open court?
options: He tore the queen's necklace from her neck
He kicked Sairandhri in front of the king
He struck the courtier Kanka across the face
He drew his sword on prince Uttara
questions.explanation: When Sairandhri fled to the court for protection, Kichaka kicked her before Virata, who dared not act against him.

questions.q: What were Kichaka's hundred-odd kinsmen called?
options: Trigartas
Maukharis
Upakichakas
Sutaputras
questions.explanation: His kinsmen, the Upakichakas, died trying to burn Sairandhri on his pyre.

questions.q: How did Queen Sudeshna assist her brother's designs on Sairandhri?
options: She sent Sairandhri to Kichaka's quarters to fetch wine
She granted Kichaka entry to the women's chambers
She told Kichaka where Sairandhri slept at night
She ordered Sairandhri to dance at his feast
questions.explanation: Yielding to her brother's pleas, Sudeshna sent the unwilling maid to his palace on the pretext of fetching wine.

questions.q: When Kichaka first laid hands on her in his palace, where did Sairandhri flee?
options: To the queen's private apartments
To the temple of Durga in the city
To the kitchens, seeking Vallabha
To the royal court where Virata sat
questions.explanation: She broke free and ran to the assembly hall, where Kichaka pursued her and kicked her before the king.

questions.q: Why did the disguised Yudhishthira and Bhima do nothing as Sairandhri was kicked in court?
options: The king had forbidden servants to quarrel
Acting openly would expose their identities in the final year of hiding
They did not recognize her through her disguise
They feared Kichaka's strength in equal combat
questions.explanation: Any open defense would have revealed the Pandavas and forced the whole exile to be served again, so Yudhishthira restrained Bhima with veiled words.

questions.q: What did Draupadi demand when she came to Bhima in the kitchen at night?
options: That the Pandavas leave Matsya at once
That Bhima reveal himself to King Virata
That Kichaka die for the insults she had suffered
That she be released from her vow of service
questions.explanation: Weeping over her humiliations, Draupadi told Bhima she could not live unless Kichaka was killed.

questions.q: What trap was set for Kichaka?
options: An invitation to a secret midnight meeting in the dance hall
A challenge to a wrestling match before the king
A drugged cup of wine at the evening feast
A forged summons to the queen's chambers
questions.explanation: Following Bhima's plan, Sairandhri pretended to yield and invited Kichaka to meet her alone in the dance hall at night.

questions.q: Who actually waited for Kichaka on the couch in the darkened hall?
options: Sairandhri, with a hidden dagger
An empty bundle of cushions and silk
Bhima, lying in wait in her place
A palace guard bribed by the queen
questions.explanation: Bhima lay on the couch in the dark, and when Kichaka reached for him the wrestling match to the death began.

questions.q: How did the Upakichakas meet their end?
options: Virata executed them for treason
They fell in the Trigarta cattle raid
They killed one another quarreling over succession
Bhima slew them when they tried to burn Sairandhri with the corpse
questions.explanation: When they seized Sairandhri to burn her on Kichaka's pyre, Bhima fell on them at the burning ground and killed them all.

questions.q: What did the Kauravas deduce from the news of Kichaka's strange death?
options: That Virata was hiding a divine champion
That only a warrior like Bhima could have crushed Kichaka so
That the Gandharvas had taken Matsya's side
That Matsya was cursed and ripe for plunder
questions.explanation: Hearing how the body was crushed, Duryodhana's council reasoned that such a death pointed to Bhima, and so to the hidden Pandavas.

questions.q: How was Kichaka's death explained in Virata's city?
options: Gandharvas had slain him for touching their wife
A rival commander had hired assassins
The gods had punished his overweening pride
He had died in a drunken brawl
questions.explanation: The city believed Sairandhri's invisible Gandharva husbands had destroyed Kichaka for his lust.

questions.q: Why had Matsya's enemies kept away from the kingdom for years?
options: The kingdom's walls were thought unbreachable
Virata's alliance with Panchala protected it
Fear of Kichaka's might shielded the realm
The Kurus had guaranteed Matsya's borders
questions.explanation: Kichaka's strength had long kept raiders at bay, which is why his death immediately invited the Trigarta and Kuru attacks.

questions.q: What did Kichaka promise Sairandhri to win her over?
options: Marriage to his eldest son
Her freedom and safe passage home
Jewels from the royal treasury
Wealth and rank as his mistress and queen
questions.explanation: Kichaka offered to make the maid his queen, promising riches, servants, and his own devotion.

questions.q: What condition did Sairandhri set for the supposed tryst?
options: That he free her from the queen's service first
That he come alone, telling no one
That he renounce his command for her sake
That he swear an oath before the sacred fire
questions.explanation: She insisted on absolute secrecy so no friend or kinsman would know, which left Kichaka alone with Bhima in the dark.

questions.q: How many of Kichaka's kinsmen died with him, by the traditional count?
options: A hundred and five
Exactly one hundred
Sixty-four
Two hundred and one
questions.explanation: The epic counts a hundred and five Upakichakas slain by Bhima at the burning ground.

questions.q: Which king had long suffered defeat and plunder at Kichaka's hands?
options: Jayadratha of Sindhu
Shalya of Madra
Susharma of Trigarta
Sudakshina of Kamboja
questions.explanation: Susharma of Trigarta had been beaten repeatedly by Kichaka, which is why he urged a raid on Matsya the moment Kichaka was dead.

questions.q: At what point in the Pandavas' hidden year did the Kichaka episode occur?
options: In the very first weeks of their service
Near the end, with only a short time left to stay concealed
Exactly at the midpoint of the year
After the year had already expired
questions.explanation: The episode came late in the thirteenth year, when discovery would have been most ruinous, with Draupadi having served some ten months.

questions.q: What assurance did Bhima give the weeping Draupadi in the kitchen?
options: That the year would soon end and Kichaka would face them in open war
That he would spirit her away to Panchala by night
That Yudhishthira would demand justice from Virata
That he would kill Kichaka the very next night at the meeting place
questions.explanation: Bhima promised to be waiting in the dance hall the following night and to crush Kichaka there in secret.

questions.q: Dragged toward the burning ground, by what names did Sairandhri cry out for rescue?
options: Jaya, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jayatsena, and Jayadbala
Kanka, Vallabha, and Granthika
Dharma, Vayu, and Indra, fathers of her lords
Chitrasena and the hosts of the Gandharvas
questions.explanation: She called her supposed Gandharva husbands by the secret names the Pandavas had agreed upon, and Bhima answered the call.

questions.q: How did Bhima reach the burning ground to save Sairandhri without being recognized?
options: He rode out openly in the king's livery
He disguised himself as a wandering ascetic
He slipped over the city wall by night and tore up a tree for a weapon
He joined the funeral procession as a mourner
questions.explanation: Bhima went over the wall unseen, uprooted a tree, and fell upon the Upakichakas like the Gandharva everyone feared.

questions.q: How did Virata's court respond to the slaughter of the Upakichakas?
options: The king demanded the Gandharvas be appeased with sacrifice
In terror of the unseen Gandharvas, the queen was told to send Sairandhri away
The army was mustered to hunt down the killers
Kanka was accused of plotting the deaths
questions.explanation: The frightened king had Sudeshna ask Sairandhri to leave, and Draupadi begged just thirteen more days, after which her Gandharvas would take her away.

questions.q: With what weapons did Bhima fight Kichaka in the dance hall?
options: A mace seized from the palace armory
A knotted cloth weighted with stones
An iron pestle from the kitchens
None; he wrestled and crushed him bare-handed
questions.explanation: The fight was a bare-handed wrestling match in the dark, ending with Bhima crushing Kichaka into a ball of flesh.
